name: i-Space (Interstellar Glory)

overview.value: i-Space, also known as Beijing Interstellar Glory Space Technology Ltd. or Interstellar Glory, is a Chinese private space technology development and space launch company based in Beijing, founded in October 2016. It develops two-stage small-satellite orbital launchers using solid-propellant rocket engines procured from CASC. In July 2019 its Hyperbola-1 rocket became the first privately developed Chinese launch vehicle to reach low Earth orbit.

focus.value: space technology development and space launch

notes: Added in the audit Phase 3 backfill (2026-07-08), verify-then-add.

i-Space's Hyperbola-1 fails on second orbital launch attempt
i-Space's Hyperbola-1 fails on third orbital launch attempt
i-Space's Hyperbola-1 fails on fourth orbital launch attempt
i-Space returns to flight with successful Hyperbola-1 launch
i-Space's Hyperbola-1 places DEAR-1 satellite in orbit
i-Space raises $99 million Series C and C+ funding round (per Payload)
